name: Fnb Coweta
description_short: FNB Coweta is a community bank in Oklahoma serving customers since 1903, offering personal checking, mortgages, business loans, and personal loans through multiple locations.
description_long: FNB Coweta is the oldest locally owned bank in Wagoner County, Oklahoma, with roots dating back to July 22, 1903, when it opened as the First National Bank of Coweta in a small frame building on North Broadway. The bank was moved to a newly constructed two-story facility at the corner of Broadway and Sycamore, where it continues to operate today under its current name (changed in 2019). Throughout its 120+ year history, FNB Coweta has positioned itself as a pillar of financial stability that has stood with customers through both prosperous and difficult times. The bank offers a full range of personal and business banking services. For personal customers, this includes checking accounts with debit/ATM cards, personal loans for various purposes (construction, real estate, home improvements), and a comprehensive suite of mortgage products. Business customers can access commercial lending for businesses of all sizes, renovation loans, and business debit cards. The bank provides mobile banking through the FNB Coweta app available on Google Play and Mac, allowing customers to manage finances remotely. Additional services include utility payment processing for City of Coweta and Lake Region Electric Company, check printing, and check cashing services. FNB Coweta distinguishes itself through its deep community roots and personalized banking approach. With the tagline "Banking Made Personal" and "Putting People First since 1903," the bank emphasizes local ownership and customer relationships. The bank operates three physical locations including a drive-through facility with extended evening and weekend hours (7:30 AM to 6 PM weekdays), making banking accessible for working customers. The main branch in Coweta and a secondary branch in Broken Arrow (Oneta Branch) provide geographic convenience to the surrounding area. As a traditional community bank, FNB Coweta is best suited for customers who value local relationships, personal service, and established financial institutions. The bank's longevity and community focus appeal to those prioritizing stability and face-to-face banking interactions. However, prospective customers should be aware that FNB Coweta operates with limited branch locations compared to national banks, which may restrict accessibility for those outside the Wagoner County and greater Oklahoma area. The bank does not advertise specific APRs, fees, or competitive rate information on its website, requiring customers to contact the institution directly for pricing details.
services: Personal checking accounts with optional debit/ATM card
Personal loans for construction, real estate, and home improvements
Mortgage loans with mortgage calculator tool
Commercial lending for businesses of various sizes
Online banking portal with secure login
Mobile banking app (Google Play and Mac)
Utility payment processing (City of Coweta and Lake Region Electric)
pros: Oldest locally owned bank in Wagoner County with 120+ years of continuous operation since 1903
Three physical locations including drive-through bank open 7:30 AM-6 PM weekdays for after-hours access
Mobile app available for both Google Play and Mac platforms enabling remote account management
Offers personal loans specifically for construction, real estate, and home improvement projects
Comprehensive mortgage services with dedicated mortgage calculator tool on website
Commercial lending available for businesses of all sizes with dedicated business services team
Accepts utility payments for City of Coweta and Lake Region Electric Company at the bank
cons: Limited geographic reach with only two main branches serving Wagoner County and Broken Arrow area
Website does not provide specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules for comparison shopping
Oneta Branch has limited posted hours (phone number only, directing customers to call for times)
No information about online account opening; customers must visit branch or call to open accounts
Limited digital services mentioned — app functionality not detailed beyond general finance management
best_for: Long-term Coweta, Oklahoma residents prioritizing community banking relationships and local ownership
Small to medium-sized business owners seeking personalized commercial lending and local support
Homebuyers in the Oklahoma area needing mortgage services with face-to-face guidance
Customers valuing banking stability and 100+ year institutional history over national chain convenience
diagnosis: FNB Coweta is best for Oklahoma residents seeking stable, community-focused banking with personalized service and a 120-year track record, particularly small business owners and homebuyers in the Wagoner County area. The main caveat is limited geographic reach outside Oklahoma and lack of transparent fee/rate information online, requiring direct customer contact for pricing comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
